verses.text: Flee, children of Benjamin, for evil rises from the north; shepherds with their flocks come to besiege Zion. The siege gathers. The enemy encamps around the city like herdsmen settling their flocks.
Flee, children of Benjamin, for evil rises from the north; shepherds with their flocks come to besiege Zion. The siege gathers. The enemy encamps around the city like herdsmen settling their flocks.
Flee, children of Benjamin, for evil rises from the north; shepherds with their flocks come to besiege Zion. The siege gathers. The enemy encamps around the city like herdsmen settling their flocks.
They cry, 'Prepare ye war... arise, let us go up at noon,' to hew trees and raise a mound against Jerusalem. The assault is eager. God's instruments press the attack on the guilty city.
They cry, 'Prepare ye war... arise, let us go up at noon,' to hew trees and raise a mound against Jerusalem. The assault is eager. God's instruments press the attack on the guilty city.
They cry, 'Prepare ye war... arise, let us go up at noon,' to hew trees and raise a mound against Jerusalem. The assault is eager. God's instruments press the attack on the guilty city.

As a fountain pours out water, so the city pours out wickedness; 'Be thou instructed, O Jerusalem, lest my soul depart from thee.' A last warning is given. God pleads even now for the city to be taught before He withdraws.

As a fountain pours out water, so the city pours out wickedness; 'Be thou instructed, O Jerusalem, lest my soul depart from thee.' A last warning is given. God pleads even now for the city to be taught before He withdraws.
The remnant shall be gleaned like a vine; to whom can Jeremiah speak, when 'their ear is uncircumcised'? They cannot hear. A people closed to God's word are ripe for the wrath he must pour out.

From least to greatest all are greedy, and prophets heal 'the hurt... slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when there is no peace.' False comfort deceives. To cry peace over an unhealed wound is the cruelty of false prophets.

'Stand ye in the ways... ask for the old paths, where is the good way, and walk therein'; but they said, 'We will not walk therein.' Wisdom calls them back. The tried, ancient ways of God are refused by a self-willed people.

'Stand ye in the ways... ask for the old paths, where is the good way, and walk therein'; but they said, 'We will not walk therein.' Wisdom calls them back. The tried, ancient ways of God are refused by a self-willed people.

God will bring evil on this people, 'the fruit of their thoughts,' for they spurned His law; even sacrifices cannot please Him. Their ruin is self-grown. The harvest of their own devices is the judgment they reap.

A cruel people comes from the north; the report makes hands feeble; 'gird thee with sackcloth... for the spoiler shall suddenly come.' Terror spreads. The faithful are called to mourn the coming desolation.

God set Jeremiah as an assayer to test His people; tried in the fire, they prove 'reprobate silver,' for the LORD has rejected them. The refining fails. A people who will not be purified are at last cast away.
